---
title: "Removals, post- de-duplication"
author: "Cindy J. Pang"
date: "2026-03-04"
format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    fontsize: 11pt
    geometry: margin=1in
    number-sections: false
    colorlinks: true
    keep-tex: false
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
rm(list=ls()) 
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(arrow)
library(ggplot2)
```

```{r}
# read and process data
removals_counts <- read_feather(here::here("data/ice-counts/removals-weekly-counts.feather"))|>
  rename(n_original = n)
removals_drop_dup <- read_feather(here::here("data/ice-counts/removals-weekly-counts-drop-duplicates.feather"))|>
  rename(n_drop_duplicates = n)

removals_comparison <- merge(removals_counts, 
                            removals_drop_dup, 
                            by = c("year_week", "week_start"), 
                            all = TRUE)

removals_comparison <- removals_comparison |>
  mutate(n_drop = n_original - n_drop_duplicates)
```

```{r}
ggplot(removals_comparison, aes(x = week_start, y = n_drop)) +
  geom_col(fill = "darkgreen") +
  labs(
    x = "Week start",
    y = "Count",
    title = "# of Dropped Observations"
  )+
  theme_minimal()
```

# Plot of Removals (post-duplication drops)  
```{r}
df <- removals_comparison |>
  arrange(week_start) |>
  mutate(
    source_files_norm = ifelse(is.na(source_files), "", source_files),
    changed = source_files_norm != lag(source_files_norm, default = first(source_files_norm)),
    seg_id = cumsum(changed),
    seg_code = paste0("S", seg_id)
  )

# segment metadata (for label placement)
segments <- df |>
  group_by(seg_id, seg_code) |>
  summarise(
    seg_start = min(week_start),
    seg_end   = max(week_start),
    seg_mid   = seg_start + as.numeric(seg_end - seg_start)/2,
    source_files = first(source_files_norm),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# vlines at segment starts (exclude first segment)
vlines <- segments |>
  arrange(seg_start) |>
  slice(-1)

# y position for segment codes (near top)
y_top <- max(df$n_original, df$n_drop_duplicates, na.rm = TRUE)

ggplot(df, aes(x = week_start)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = n_drop_duplicates), linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(
    data = vlines,
    aes(xintercept = as.numeric(seg_start)),
    linetype = "dashed",
    alpha = 0.6,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_label(
    data = segments,
    aes(x = seg_mid, y = y_top, label = seg_code),
    vjust = -0.2,
    size = 3,
    label.size = 0.2,
    alpha = 0.95,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.15))) +
  labs(
    title = "Weekly Removals with Data Source Change Points",
    subtitle = "See Segment Codes (SX) to see time periods",
    x = "Week start",
    y = "Counts",
    color = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

segments_print <- segments |>
  select(seg_code, seg_start, seg_end, source_files)
knitr::kable(segments_print, col.names = c("Segment Code", "Start Date", "End Date", "Source Files"))
```

```{r}
ggplot(removals_comparison, aes(x = week_start, y = n_original)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(
    title = "Original Removal Counts (without de-duplication)", 
    x = "Week start", 
    y = "Counts"
  )+
  theme_minimal()
```

# Comparison Table  
```{r}
removals_comparison_to_print <- removals_comparison |>
  select(year_week, week_start, n_original, n_drop_duplicates, n_drop,source_files)
knitr::kable(removals_comparison_to_print, col.names = c("Year-Week", "Week Start", "Original Counts", "De-duplicated Counts", "# Dropped Observations", "Source Files"))
```
